title: Drift Taxonomy Record
description: 7-field record shape for drift phenomena. Locked by drift-mission.tex Stage-3 deliverable D1. One row per drift phenomenon; populated by Wave 1 module authors and synthesized in Wave 2.

records.description: Array of drift-phenomenon records. Minimum 10 for Wave-2 unified taxonomy requirement (DRIFT-04).

phenomenon.description: Short name of the drift phenomenon (e.g. 'Semantic drift', 'Task drift from prompt injection').

surface.description: Which of the three drift surfaces (Module A/B/C) or cross-surface (Module D) this phenomenon belongs to.

metric.description: The named measurement used (e.g. 'SD score on atomic facts', 'Activation-delta probe AUROC').

typical_magnitude.description: The reported effect size with units (e.g. '0.77-0.79 across major LLMs', '30pp BoolQ drop').

citation_id.description: cite_key from sources/index.bib (e.g. 'spataru2024sd', 'abdelnabi2024taskdrift'). Must resolve in meta.json.

mitigations.description: List of mitigations from the cited paper or related work. Each mitigation names a technique + its reported effect.

couplings.description: Cross-surface coupling claims linking this phenomenon to others (e.g. 'knowledge->retrieval via stale index'). Filled in Wave 2 synthesis.

records.metric: Activation-delta probe ROC AUC (white-box linear classifier on hidden-state deltas before vs after external content)
records.typical_magnitude: Near-perfect ROC AUC on OOD test sets (paper reports >0.99)

mitigations.name: Activation-delta probe
mitigations.effect: Detects task drift at near-perfect OOD ROC AUC; probe-only trained, base LLM unchanged

mitigations.name: TraceShield inference filter (prompt-injection adjacent)
mitigations.effect: 40% baseline alignment drift reduced to 6.2% on ADB when deployed full-stack; filter refuses high-BCI completions

couplings.claim: Task drift originates from external content injected via retrieval pipelines; detection at the activation boundary is simultaneously a retrieval-drift signal

records.metric: Belief Conflict Index (BCI) from suffix-array-matched training spans; Alignment Drift Benchmark (ADB)
records.typical_magnitude: Baseline ADB drift >40%; full-stack intervention reduces to 6.2%; up to 85% reduction combined; utility delta <0.2

mitigations.name: TraceShield (inference filter)
mitigations.effect: Refuses completions with high-BCI spans; contributes to 40%->6.2% ADB drift reduction full-stack

mitigations.name: Contrastive Belief Deconfliction
mitigations.effect: DPO-style penalty on high-BCI continuations; up to 85% reduction combined with TraceShield + Prov-Decode

mitigations.name: Prov-Decode
mitigations.effect: Provenance-aware decoding vetoes beam expansions predicted to yield high-BCI spans; subsumed in full-stack

couplings.claim: TraceAlign ties alignment-drift failures to memorized pretraining content, making the knowledge surface the upstream cause of the alignment surface under adversarial pressure

records.metric: Turn-wise KL divergence between test model predictive distribution and goal-consistent reference model
records.typical_magnitude: Drift reaches stable finite-noise equilibrium rather than runaway decay; reminder interventions shift equilibrium downward

mitigations.name: Periodic goal reminders
mitigations.effect: Reliably shifts stable KL equilibrium downward in synthetic rewriting tasks + tau-Bench user-agent sims

mitigations.name: Stochastic recurrence with restoring force (theoretical)
mitigations.effect: Theoretical account predicts reminder-intervention effect magnitude; used as basis for designing reminder cadence

couplings.claim: Context drift loses/corrupts information in the active context; the loss surface intersects knowledge drift when corrupted context causes factual regression

records.phenomenon: Goal drift under prolonged adversarial pressure
records.metric: End-of-run stated-goal alignment score (agent-reported vs system-assigned) correlated with measured goal-drift score
records.typical_magnitude: Claude 3.5 Sonnet never states misaligned goal; Claude 3.5 Haiku + GPT-4o state hybrid/misaligned more frequently; GPT-4o mini states misaligned goal in majority of runs

mitigations.name: Self-report auditing (Finding 9)
mitigations.effect: Models' goal self-reports correlate strongly with measured drift; auditing self-reports is a cheap drift signal

mitigations.name: Goal reminders (cross-paper mitigation)
mitigations.effect: Dongre et al. show periodic goal reminders shift KL equilibrium downward; this transfers to goaldrift2025's prompt-based goal setting

couplings.claim: Intrinsic (training-acquired) goal drift cited as future work via greenblatt2024intrinsic and betley2024emergent; bridges into the knowledge surface by locating goal representation in weights not prompts

records.metric: Agent Stability Index (ASI): twelve-dimensional composite measuring behavior, decision quality, inter-agent coherence over extended interaction
records.typical_magnitude: Adaptive Behavioral Anchoring solo: 70.4% drift reduction; combined stack (Anchoring + EMC summarization + third intervention): 81.5% reduction at 23% compute overhead and 9% extended completion time

mitigations.name: Adaptive Behavioral Anchoring
mitigations.effect: 70.4% drift reduction solo in simulated enterprise-finance experiments

mitigations.name: Anchoring + EMC summarization + third intervention stack
mitigations.effect: 81.5% drift reduction at 23% compute overhead and 9% extended completion time

couplings.claim: Agent drift occurs without parameter updates; the failure originates in contextual conditioning and sampling, so retrieval-surface context-shaping interventions transfer directly

records.metric: Contextualized Role Adherence Score (CRAS) + tri-axial head-drift score (magnitude, directional orientation, distributional reshaping)
records.typical_magnitude: Focal attention heads concentrate in middle layers; LoRA + token-weighted DPO on focal layers yields MAS role-adherence gains

mitigations.name: SAIL (Surgical Alignment of Instruction Layers) LoRA adapters
mitigations.effect: LoRA on focal mid-layer attention heads with token-weighted DPO objective; re-confirms subset-head criticality from Michel et al. 2019

mitigations.name: Attention-head pruning ancestor
mitigations.effect: Classical finding that only a subset of attention heads are functionally critical; methodology ancestor for SAIL focal-head localization

couplings.claim: Mid-layer focal heads encode instruction-arbitration signals; the same depth locus is where intermediate-representation hallucination probes operate (DRIFT 2026 in Module A), suggesting a shared mid-depth intervention surface

records.phenomenon: Natural context drift vs pretraining snapshot (retrieval surface reading)
records.metric: QA accuracy vs paragraph-level semantic similarity between edited and pretraining-era passages (BoolQ)
records.typical_magnitude: Over 30pp BoolQ drop between highest- and lowest-similarity bins; regression slopes >70 across several LLMs; human annotators do not degrade

mitigations.name: Versioned index with source timestamp
mitigations.effect: Fresh index paired with source-version fingerprint so retrievers can flag generator-prior divergence

mitigations.name: Time-aware retrieval (Chronos-style)
mitigations.effect: Event Evolution Graph enables temporally consistent understanding without model retraining; reduces similarity-bin accuracy gap

couplings.claim: The same finding anchors Module A (knowledge surface): a stale parametric prior on the generator side interacts with a fresh index on the retrieval side to produce accuracy loss

records.phenomenon: Response Semantic Drift in diffusion-LM RAG
records.metric: Qualitative: copy-rate from context vs query-semantic precision across iterative denoising steps (no published RSD scalar)
records.typical_magnitude: Qualitatively high copy-rate paired with low query-precision; drift shape differs from autoregressive LM drift on identical retrievals

mitigations.name: Query-semantic alignment across denoising
mitigations.effect: Denoising strategies that preserve alignment to the query mitigate RSD; specific mechanism qualitative in source paper

mitigations.name: Architecture-specific RAG discipline
mitigations.effect: RAG discipline cannot be architecture-agnostic; diffusion LMs need a second drift axis (denoising alignment) audited

couplings.claim: Generator architecture is a knowledge-surface variable; diffusion vs autoregressive produces different drift shapes on identical grounding

records.phenomenon: Continuous knowledge drift with Event Evolution Graph (Chronos)
records.metric: Qualitative: catastrophic forgetting and temporal inconsistency on real-world dynamic-event benchmark (no headline accuracy delta cited in drift-mission.tex)
records.typical_magnitude: Qualitative: vanilla RAG and learning-based baselines fail on the benchmark; Chronos time-aware retrieval achieves temporal consistency without retraining

mitigations.name: Event Evolution Graph (Chronos)
mitigations.effect: Progressive temporal organization of retrieved evidence enables temporally consistent understanding without model retraining

mitigations.name: Time-aware retriever signals (not prompt-side)
mitigations.effect: Temporal signals embedded in retrieval itself rather than added as prompt-side scaffolding; prevents collapsed snapshots in index

couplings.claim: Temporal inconsistency in retrieval propagates to factual drift in generation; the couple runs through the event-evolution graph as the shared temporal scaffolding

records.metric: Semantic Grounding Index (SGI): ratio of angular distances response->question vs response->context on unit hypersphere
records.typical_magnitude: Cohen's d 0.92-1.28 across 5 embedding models on HaluEval (n=5000); effect rises monotonically from d=0.61 at low Q-C separation to 1.27 at high separation; AUC 0.72->0.83 correspondingly

mitigations.name: SGI geometric audit
mitigations.effect: Response-question-context angular triangle audit separates hallucination from faithful grounding at Cohen's d >=0.9

mitigations.name: Grounding ablation test (context-removal)
mitigations.effect: Redact retrieved context; if original answer is regenerated, grounding was weak regardless of citations emitted

couplings.claim: Semantic laziness parallels retrieval laziness (raggov2025): generator-side laziness inside the response mirrors retriever-side laziness outside it, with both compromising grounding discipline

records.phenomenon: Query drift and retrieval laziness in iterative RAG
records.metric: Qualitative: reformulation distance from original query and re-retrieval rate across iteration turns (no headline accuracy delta cited in drift-mission.tex)
records.typical_magnitude: Qualitative: reformulated queries wander from the original information need; agent stops re-retrieving or retrieves superficial chunks once any response at all is possible

mitigations.name: Query-drift gate with reformulation-distance monitor
mitigations.effect: Detect when reasoning-agent reformulations exceed a distance budget from original query; triggers re-grounding

mitigations.name: Re-retrieval coverage threshold
mitigations.effect: Trigger mandatory re-retrieval whenever context coverage falls below a named threshold, preventing laziness-driven shortcut completions

couplings.claim: Retrieval laziness and semantic laziness (SGI) are complementary pressures on grounding: retriever outside the generator stops asking; generator inside stops departing toward context

records.metric: Qualitative: accuracy variance across delimiter/marker/placement choices with semantic content held constant; attention-entropy profile across long contexts (no headline numerics surfaced in drift-mission.tex per 684.1 advisory)
records.typical_magnitude: Qualitative: superficial format choices induce substantial accuracy swings; long context dilutes attention entropy and degrades focus on critical segments

mitigations.name: Contextual Normalization
mitigations.effect: Lightweight adaptive strategy that standardizes context representations before generation; removes incidental format-driven drift

mitigations.name: Balanced-entropy engineering (BEE-RAG)
mitigations.effect: Context-entropy watch identifies collapse points where critical-segment focus drops below threshold; redistribution preserves focus on high-relevance chunks

mitigations.name: Context-length budget
mitigations.effect: Context length amplifies drift across turns; treating budget as drift-control knob complements BEE-RAG attention-entropy mechanism

couplings.claim: Context-length coupling to drift in multi-turn interactions (contextlen2025drift) intersects Dongre et al.'s alignment-surface KL equilibrium: both identify context as the drift-shaping substrate across turns

records.phenomenon: RAG-index concept-drift detection (classical ML tooling transfer)
records.metric: Reference-distribution + drift-metric + alarm-threshold (DriftLens idiom); specialization pattern from D3Bench microbenchmark (Evidently AI, NannyML, Alibi-Detect)
records.typical_magnitude: Qualitative: Evidently AI leads on general data-drift detection; NannyML pinpoints timing of shifts and their downstream predictive-accuracy effect; smart-building->RAG transfer is proposed, not established

mitigations.name: DriftLens unsupervised drift detection
mitigations.effect: Real-time concept-drift detection from deep-learning representations; index distribution serves as reference, new retrievals as window, alarm threshold triggers on divergence

mitigations.name: D3Bench tool comparison (Evidently AI, NannyML, Alibi-Detect)
mitigations.effect: Microbenchmark finds tool specialization; Evidently AI general detection, NannyML timing pinpointing; both idioms transfer to RAG-index monitoring

couplings.claim: An index that drifts from its source is the retrieval-surface analog of a pretraining prior drifting from the current world; the same tooling detects both under the reference/metric/threshold trinity

records.metric: Semantic Drift (SD) score on atomic facts; FActScore pipeline on 500-biography corpus
records.typical_magnitude: SD score 0.77-0.79 across six LLMs (Falcon-7B/40B, text-davinci-003, GPT-3.5, GPT-4, LLaMA-2-70B); random-permutation p<0.02; drift falls within first 10% of facts in 37% of paragraphs

mitigations.name: Oracle early-stop at drift point
mitigations.effect: FActScore 44.6% -> 81.7% on LLaMA-2-70B; removes 92% of incorrect facts at cost of 58% of correct facts

mitigations.name: EOS-token top-k early-stop heuristic
mitigations.effect: 70% factuality via top-k EOS encouragement; recovers a fraction of the oracle gain at near-zero decode-time cost

mitigations.name: SelfCheck-BERTScore early-stop signal
mitigations.effect: Tradeoff curve anchored at 70%+ accuracy; SC-BERTScore threshold used as stopping signal

mitigations.name: SC-BERTScore resample-then-rerank
mitigations.effect: +8.7pp factuality (to 53.3%) via N-sample resample with SC-BERTScore rerank; composes additively with early-stop

couplings.claim: Tool-call / Toolformer-style external QA produced near-zero improvement in Spataru 2024 -- attributed to the model's inability to gate retrieval against need; this couples semantic drift to the retrieval surface via the retrieval-gating failure mode

records.phenomenon: Late-generation factuality decay (FActScore vs stopping position)
records.metric: FActScore on 500-biography long-form generations vs stopping position (end-of-paragraph, oracle drift point, SC-BERTScore threshold)
records.typical_magnitude: Baseline 44.6% FActScore on LLaMA-2-70B at natural end-of-generation; oracle-stop at drift point raises to 81.7% (+37.1pp absolute)

mitigations.name: Oracle early-stop
mitigations.effect: 44.6% -> 81.7% FActScore; removes 92% of incorrect and 58% of correct facts

mitigations.name: EOS-encouragement + SelfCheck-BERTScore hybrid
mitigations.effect: 70%+ factuality via practical stopping signals; recovers a substantial fraction of the oracle gain

couplings.claim: Oracle-stop gain of 37.1pp is not achievable via external retrieval in Spataru's setting; the knowledge-drift fix is truncation, not retrieval -- a structural hint that retrieval discipline (Module C) is insufficient for this phenomenon

records.metric: Uncertainty deltas (entropy, perplexity, token probability) under clean vs false-info-injection vs random-info-injection prompt conditions
records.typical_magnitude: +56.6% uncertainty on first exposure to false information; -52.8% uncertainty on repeated exposure (below untainted baseline) -- model has internalized the lie; signature visible across GPT-4o, GPT-3.5, LLaMA-2-13B, Mistral-7B

mitigations.name: Prompt-hygiene / injection filtering
mitigations.effect: Prevents first-exposure uncertainty spike by excluding false-info prompts at the input boundary (upstream mitigation implied by the paper's methodology)

mitigations.name: Repeated-exposure screening
mitigations.effect: Detect -52.8% uncertainty drop (sub-baseline confidence) as internalized-lie signature; flags second-stage failures not caught by first-stage injection filter

couplings.claim: A retrieval index that serves prior false claims reproduces the same uncertainty dynamics as direct prompt injection; stale-index reactivation is the retrieval-surface analog of Fastowski's first-exposure spike

records.phenomenon: Natural context drift against pretraining snapshot (knowledge reading)
records.metric: BoolQ accuracy vs paragraph-level semantic-similarity bin between edited passage and pretraining-era version; across six QA datasets and eight LLMs with public training data
records.typical_magnitude: >30pp BoolQ accuracy drop between highest- and lowest-similarity bins; regression slopes exceed 70 across several LLMs; human annotators do not show the degradation

mitigations.name: Recency-aware retrieval (handed to Module C)
mitigations.effect: Rewriting pretraining-form passages toward their current-form variants should recover a bounded fraction of the >30pp gap; mitigation path delegated to the retrieval surface

mitigations.name: Human-annotator baseline as control
mitigations.effect: Humans do not degrade on edited passages at the same magnitude; the gap indexes LLM reliance on pretraining surface form, which is the intervention target

couplings.claim: The passages that degrade model accuracy are retrieval targets in a typical RAG pipeline; Wu et al.'s >30pp BoolQ drop is simultaneously a retrieval-drift signal, establishing a direct knowledge -> retrieval coupling

records.metric: Margin-based contrastive alignment between hidden-state representations and factual encoder embeddings, per layer; F1 / AUROC / clustering accuracy on TruthfulQA + synthetic factual-hallucination corpus
records.typical_magnitude: F1=0.92, AUROC=0.96, clustering accuracy=0.89 on evaluated corpus; factual responses preserve stable alignment across depth; hallucinations exhibit pronounced late-layer divergence from the truth manifold

mitigations.name: Layer-wise Semantic Dynamics (LSD) probe
mitigations.effect: Intrinsic hallucination detection via margin-based contrastive alignment; F1=0.92 / AUROC=0.96 on evaluated corpus (single-author, custom synthetic data alongside TruthfulQA -- framed as reported-on-corpus, not benchmark-standard)

mitigations.name: Late-layer intervention target
mitigations.effect: Mechanistic story (early=syntax, middle=semantic integration, late=factual refinement) localizes the failure to late-layer refinement, providing a concrete layer-index target for mitigation

couplings.claim: Late-layer divergence is the depth locus where instruction-arbitration focal heads also operate (SAIL 2025 in Module B); shared mid-to-late-layer intervention surface suggests coordinated mitigation across knowledge + alignment surfaces

records.metric: Lightweight probe over intermediate-layer representations; AUROC on QA benchmarks across multiple LLM families
records.typical_magnitude: SOTA AUROC on 10 of 12 settings across four QA benchmarks and three LLM families; up to +13 AUROC points over prior methods; <0.1% computational overhead running in parallel with decoding

mitigations.name: Intermediate-representation probe
mitigations.effect: Detects hallucination before generation completes at <0.1% overhead; first sub-1% overhead mitigation in the Module-A surface

mitigations.name: Probe-based router (default -> stronger model)
mitigations.effect: Confident queries route to default backbone; uncertain queries delegated to a stronger model; first practical probe-routing story in the knowledge-drift corpus

mitigations.name: Intermediate-representation faithfulness motivation
mitigations.effect: Motivating intuition: intermediate representations encode reasoning signals not always faithfully expressed in final-layer output; used for motivation only (review_status=partial on Phase 684.1 editorial review)

couplings.claim: Probe-based routing delegates uncertain queries to a stronger model; this intersects the alignment-drift surface whenever the stronger model has different alignment properties -- a routing decision is simultaneously an alignment decision
